one_line_description: B2B customer support platform unifying Slack, email, and in-app support with account management.
auth_summary: API tokens created in-app; only Admin users can create API tokens. Public docs confirm token-based access but do not publicly detail the header format; the official MCP server separately uses OAuth 2.0 (AuthKit).
access_notes: No free trial or free plan; all tiers are annual contracts starting ~$59/seat/month (3-seat minimum), typically sold via demo. Once a customer, an admin can self-serve create API tokens.
api_surface_summary: REST API covering issues, accounts, contacts, messages, attachments, knowledge base, macros, tags, tasks/projects, teams, users/roles, custom fields/objects, surveys, and audit logs.
main_blocker: No self-serve or trial account: getting API credentials requires becoming a paying Pylon customer via a sales-led annual contract.
agent_notes: API reference index confirms broad resource coverage, but exact auth header format was not visible in the fetched docs pages (docs use a dynamic query system). Official hosted MCP server exists at mcp.usepylon.com with OAuth. Pricing/trial details came partly from third-party pricing roundups; Pylon reportedly offers occasional limited trial access on request.
